signals.raw: South Korea's annual CPI inflation rose to 2.2% YoY in March 2026 (from 2.0% in February), below market consensus of 2.3-2.4% due to government measures curbing oil-driven price hikes | NO | HIGH confidence**[1][2]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Key details:** This breaking CPI data, released around April 2026, showed monthly prices up 0
signals.raw: Key details:** This breaking CPI data, released around April 2026, showed monthly prices up 0.3% (vs. 0.6% expected), with core inflation at 2.2% YoY (down slightly from February). Oil prices drove the uptick, but fuel caps and food vouchers limited impact; food prices fell to 0.5% YoY. Bank of Korea expected to hold rates at 2.5% in April, adopting wait-and-see stance amid contained pressures and

signals.raw: Prediction market relevance:** Lower-than-expected print reduces odds of rate hikes or policy shifts, potentially moving markets toward stable 2% target outlook (vs. prior forecasts of 2.6% quarterly end).[2] No resolutions on Middle East shocks yet, per recent analyses.[5][7]
